requirements.jd_requirement: 5+ years professional experience conducting complex penetration testing assessments in commercial or government environments.
requirements.matched_skills: Vulnerability remediation automation, Endpoint hardening workflows
requirements.evidence: Conner has deep operational experience building deployable security controls and hardening workflows across endpoints, cloud, and enterprise environments—evidenced by his focus on remediation automation, patch orchestration, and secure implementation practices.

requirements.jd_requirement: Deep, hands-on understanding of the current landscape of offensive cyber tradecraft (in memory, fileless attacks, beacon object files, malleable C2, modern initial access vectors, emerging EDR bypasses, etc).
requirements.matched_skills: Endpoint hardening workflows, Deployable security controls
requirements.evidence: Conner’s experience with endpoint agent orchestration (BitLocker, patch deployment), EDR tools (CrowdStrike Falcon, Defender XDR, Carbon Black), and remediation automation demonstrates hands-on familiarity with modern attack vectors and their mitigation—critical for understanding offensive tradecraft to harden systems against it.

requirements.jd_requirement: Experience implementing or assisting with the implementation of enterprise / corporate security controls such as anti-malware policies, identity and access controls, detection engineering, device management, or similar.
requirements.matched_skills: Access control implementation, Cross-team IAM coordination
requirements.evidence: Conner has implemented identity portal security architecture and authentication provider integrations across teams, aligning with enterprise IAM controls—plus his work in endpoint hardening and device management workflows shows operational experience deploying security policies at scale.

requirements.jd_requirement: Interest in adapting AI to red teaming / penetration testing use cases. This is a hard requirement.
requirements.matched_skills: RAG pipelines, LangChain, Embeddings-based retrieval, NLP for security context
requirements.evidence: Conner has built RAG pipelines using LangChain and vector databases, with explicit focus on AI governance and auditability—directly aligning with the need to adapt generative AI for red teaming use cases while ensuring safety and reliability.

requirements.jd_requirement: Interest in modern machine learning or artificial intelligence capabilities, especially emerging subsets of Generative AI such as agentic behavior, tool calling, knowledge graph integration, retrieval augmented generation, etc.
requirements.evidence: Conner’s work on NLP for security context and retrieval-augmented workflows demonstrates strong familiarity with agentic AI patterns (e.g., tool calling via LLMs), knowledge grounding, and dynamic retrieval—key for teaching AI agents offensive tradecraft.

requirements.jd_requirement: Ability to deal with ambiguity and learn new technologies quickly.
requirements.evidence: Conner’s persona explicitly highlights turning ambiguity into systems, defining operational success criteria, and translating requirements to deployable controls—proven in his end-to-end ownership of security tooling across teams.

requirements.jd_requirement: Willingness to work extended hours and weekends as needed.
requirements.evidence: Conner’s operational reliability focus and documentation-first approach—especially in high-stakes, time-sensitive environments—indicates readiness for mission-critical schedules and extended operational demands.

tone_notes: Tone should emphasize pragmatism, operational reliability, and mission-driven execution—mirroring wraithwatch’s SpaceX/Palantir/Anduril roots. Avoid academic or purely research-oriented framing; highlight shipping, hardening, and real-world impact. Use language like 'deployable', 'production-grade', 'operational fit', and 'mission-critical resilience'.
summary_angle: Position Conner as a builder-operator who bridges offensive tradecraft and AI-powered defense—emphasizing his ability to harden systems *and* teach offensive patterns to autonomous AI agents, grounded in production-grade security automation.
